prose:
**Aniline frontier-orbital result** — Property: HOMO–LUMO frontier-orbital gap. Level of theory: DFT tier `standard` using **B3LYP/def2-TZVP** in PySCF; I also passed the explicit `--functional b3lyp` and `--basis def2-tzvp` requested. Density fitting was not enabled. Geometry: fixed, user-supplied XYZ at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz` (single-point orbital calculation; no geometry optimization in this run). Environment: **gas phase**. Charge/multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1. SCF convergence: converged in 9 cycles. Software: PySCF (version not reported by the tool).

Headline result: **HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.3048177 eV**.

Additional orbital values returned by chemkit: HOMO = -5.553772 eV; LUMO = -0.2489544 eV. Total electronic energy = -287.71578 hartree = -7829.1453 eV.

Warnings: no warnings were reported by the chemkit result.

Integrity verdict: `trustworthy: true`.

Exact chemkit invocation returned by the tool: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --stdout json /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpotsuey8d/live.json`.

Note on live log: the chemkit API result did not include a concrete `.out` log path, so I cannot truthfully name one beyond the engine’s stated live `frontier_<timestamp>.out` convention.
